issues.display_title: Issue #4072: Handle Issue Infinite "Processing Images" Modal on Upload (Images Save to DB Successfully)
issues.main_description_html:
Description

A bug was reported where uploading a panoramic image to a specific tour results in an infinite loading modal (displaying "Przetwarzanie obrazów" / "Processing images").

The issue is a state-resolution mismatch: the upload process actually succeeds on the backend. The image is written to the database and becomes visible on the frontend if the user forcefully stops the process and manually reloads the page. However, the frontend modal fails to catch the success response/callback, causing it to spin indefinitely.

Specific Test Environment Data

  • Target Tour Moderation ID: 3371492
  • Test Level: A level named "tes" has been created specifically for debugging this upload issue.
  • Note: The reporter mentioned this issue seems isolated to this specific tour.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Uploading a pano to the specified tour (moderation_id=3371492, level "tes") successfully completes the process.
  2. The "Processing images" modal automatically closes upon successful database write.
  3. The newly uploaded image immediately appears in the UI without requiring a manual page refresh.

Test Scenario (for Tester / QA)

Preconditions

  • Logged in to an account with access to tour moderation_id=3371492.
  • Tour 3371492 is open and the level "tes" exists.
  • Have at least one valid 360° panoramic image ready to upload.

Scenario 1 — Happy path (the bug being fixed)

  1. Open tour moderation_id=3371492.
  2. Select level "tes".
  3. Upload a panoramic image.
  4. Observe the "Processing images" / "Przetwarzanie obrazów" modal appear.
  5. ✅ Expected: The modal closes automatically once the upload/DB write completes (no manual stop needed).
  6. ✅ Expected: The uploaded image appears in the UI immediately — without refreshing the page.
  7. ❌ Bug (before fix): Modal spins indefinitely; image only shows after force-stop + manual page reload.

Scenario 2 — Verify DB persistence

  1. After Scenario 1 completes, reload the page manually.
  2. ✅ Expected: The image is still present (confirming it was correctly written to the DB and the UI state matched the backend, not a duplicate or a phantom entry).

Scenario 3 — Regression check (other tours/levels)

  1. Repeat the upload flow on a different tour and a different level.
  2. ✅ Expected: Upload still works correctly and the modal closes as normal — the fix did not break the general upload flow.

Scenario 4 — Multiple / sequential uploads

  1. On tour 3371492, level "tes", upload several panoramas (one after another or in batch).
  2. ✅ Expected: Each upload resolves correctly, the modal closes for each, and all images appear without refresh.

Negative / edge checks

  • Upload an invalid file (non-panorama / corrupt): ✅ modal should close with a clear error, not hang.
  • Slow network: ✅ modal stays until completion, then closes — it must not get permanently stuck waiting for the callback.

Pass criteria: All scenarios above behave as the ✅ Expected results, and none reproduce the infinite-modal behavior.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §2.2 (Upload panoramas workflow) and §4 (Tour creation & upload).

Demo from Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #4073: Blur Section UI Refinements & AI Feature Cleanup
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Alongside the backend fixes for the blur processing, several UI updates are required on the Blur section interface (Anonymize 360 Images) to improve usability and remove non-functional features.

Sub-tasks

  • Relocate Button: Move the "Manual Blur" button to the right side of the action bar (align it next to the "Preview Tour" and "Apply AI Blur" buttons).
  • Remove AI License Plate Blurring: Completely remove the "AI License plate blurring" option/toggle from the AI section. The feature is currently underperforming and non-functional.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The "Manual Blur" button is correctly positioned on the right (next to "Preview Tour" and "Apply AI Blur").
  2. Images with existing manual blur entries in the 8_blooring table display a distinct blue icon on their UI cards.
  3. The AI License plate blurring option is no longer visible to users anywhere in the UI.

Reference (annotated screenshot)

  • "Manual Blur" button → move it to the right, next to "Preview Tour" and "Apply AI Blur".
  • Image thumbnails → if an image has manual blur in the 8_blooring table, add the (blue) icon from the button onto the image card.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §2.3 / §4 (Tour editor: face/plate blurring).

Demo from Developer

Description

Integrate the Manual Blur API into the dashboard's Anonymize 360 Images flow.

This covers wiring the frontend Manual Blur functionality to its backend API endpoints — submitting manually selected blur areas, persisting them (e.g. to the 8_blooring table), and reflecting blur status in the UI.

Scope

  • Integrate the Manual Blur API endpoint(s) for saving/applying manual blur data.
  • Send manually selected blur areas (from the 360 Image Connector / Blur Selected flow) to the API.
  • Handle API responses/callbacks correctly (success, error, processing states).
  • Reflect blur status in the UI after a successful API call (no manual refresh required).

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Manual blur selections are submitted to and persisted via the API.
  2. API success/error responses are handled gracefully in the UI (no stuck/indefinite states).
  3. Blur status is accurately reflected in the UI after integration.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Tour editor: face/plate blurring). Related to #4072, #4073, #4074 (blur work).
